subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Highly coherent logical progression from personal anecdote through definitions, exploration, proof and application
Exceptional organization via clear introduction, numbered sections/subsections, integrated tables and figures
Effective use of signposting and appendices allowing the reader to navigate the document effortlessly
weaknesses: Occasional digressions (lengthy figure captions, photographs) slightly reduce conciseness
Some tables and figures could benefit from improved labelling (e.g. units, individual subâfigure captions)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands achieved band 4 (coherence/logical development; organization/structure) and one strand band 3 (conciseness with minor repetition). The work is highly coherent and exceptionally well organized, meriting the top mark of 4 despite small areas for tightening captions and labels.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of mathematical language and notation throughout (e.g. l_k, induction symbols)
Clear definitions of variables, indices and constants early on
Effective use of multiple representations (tables of values, photographs) to support algebraic arguments
weaknesses: Some handâdrawn diagrams lack scale labels or dimension markers, weakening the link to algebra
Occasional typographical errors in subscripts and inconsistent notation reduce precision
Units are not always clarified (e.g. inside logarithms)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: One strand achieved band 4 for notation, and two strands band 3 for representations and logical clarity. While the notation is mostly consistent, minor lapses in diagram labelling and typographical precision limit the mark to 3.

strengths: Outstanding evidence of independent thinking in choosing and modelling a childhood curiosity
Highly personal approach through original experiments, photographs and measurements
Good creativity and initiative in comparing paper types and reflecting on model imperfections
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques (geometric growth and induction) are standard at HL level, so creativity is not exceptional throughout
Could more tightly link anecdotal motivation to specific mathematical aims
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands were band 3 (independent thinking; personal approach) and one strand band 2 (creativity/initiative). Overall the exploration demonstrates highly personal engagement and strong initiative, justifying the maximum mark of 3.

strengths: Substantial critical reflection on model assumptions and observed discrepancies throughout
Deep and insightful analysis of results and process, linking mathematical and experimental findings
Coherent evaluation that restates core findings and limitations
weaknesses: Evaluation of future extensions and broader implications is limited
Could outline specific next steps (e.g. parameter sensitivity, alternative fold geometries)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands were band 3 (depth of reflection; critical analysis) and one strand band 2 (evaluation of outcomes). The consistent, deep reflection and analysis earn the top mark of 3, despite a need for more comprehensive future-oriented evaluation.

strengths: Mathematics is expertly applied at HL standard (geometric sequences, logarithms, induction) and remains fully relevant to the problem
Proofs and derivations are comprehensive and linked to experimental validation
General formulae and models are derived and used to interpret real data, demonstrating expert command
weaknesses: Minor typographical errors (e.g. missing denominator) occur in a few displayed formulae
Elliptical fold model is only mentioned verbally; a formal derivation would strengthen the modelling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands were band 6 (relevance; level appropriateness) and one strand band 5 (understanding and accuracy due to small errors). The work goes beyond standard HL requirements with expert, fully correct mathematics in context, meriting the maximum mark of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations generally precede the algebra, and conclusions are stated in words, showing good logical communication.  Minor lapses (e.g. mis-typed subscripts, a stray âÎ±â instead of âaâ) prevent it from being flawless, so a solid band 3 is warranted.

Derivations are largely correct and elegantly handled; minor slips (occasional typographical errors, a missing denominator in one displayed formula) cause negligible impact on results.  Understanding is thorough, but the small inaccuracies preclude a perfect score, so band 5 is most accurate.

The student supplies algebraic derivations, tables of values and several photographs; however, some diagrams are hand-drawn and not always labelled with scales, so the link between representation and algebra is occasionally weaker.  Use of multiple representations is effective but not âhighly effectiveâ.

The development moves smoothly from a personal anecdote to clear definitions, step-by-step exploration, generalisation, proof and application.  Each mathematical section builds logically on the previous one, and signposting (e.g. â2.2 Formulatingâ¦â, â2.3 Proof by inductionâ¦â) ensures the reader never loses the thread.  Occasional digressions (photographs or lengthy figure captions) do not interrupt the main argument.  Hence the work is highly coherent with excellent logical progression.

Using semicircular versus elliptical folds, comparing three paper types and reflecting on model imperfections show creativity, yet the mathematics itself (geometric growth, induction) is fairly standard at HL, so the strand fits better in the âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ band.

The student critically analyses assumptions (constant radius, perfect alignment) and the effect of parameters on maximum folds, supporting claims with calculated and experimental data â demonstrating deep, insightful analysis.

Outcomes are compared with experiments; limitations are noted and potential improvements suggested (elliptical model).  While thoughtful, there is limited discussion of future extensions or broader implications, so band 2 fits better than band 3.

Reflection appears throughout and in a dedicated evaluation; the student identifies why the model fails (idealised semicircle, fold mis-alignment) and links this to observed discrepancies, evidencing substantial reflection.

Variables, indices and constants are defined early; notation such as $l_k$, $t_0$, $C_{2k-1}$ is used consistently; proofs employ standard symbols ( â´, âµ, â¡ ).  Mathematical language (geometric sequence, induction, logarithm) is accurate throughout, justifying the top band.
The narrative is strongly personal: the student measures her own paper, photographs each fold and justifies modelling choices.  The approach is clearly her own and highly individual.

The bulk of the writing is relevant, but some repetition (e.g. restating assumptions about semicircular folds) and very detailed derivations that are later duplicated in the appendix reduce conciseness.  Overall it remains mostly tight, yet not âhighly conciseâ; therefore band 3 is appropriate.

Geometric sequences, logarithms, proof by induction, logarithmic manipulation and modelling with parameters are all fully relevant and expertly employed to develop and verify a general formula; hence the highest band is justified.
Choosing a childhood curiosity, designing experiments, and independently constructing a mathematical model with induction displays outstanding independent thinking.

A clear introduction, numbered sections, sub-sections and a concluding evaluation give the exploration an unmistakable structure.  Tables, figures and formulae are integrated and referenced correctly, and appendices are used to separate lengthy proofs.  The reader can quickly locate any part of the investigation, demonstrating exceptional organisation.
The mathematics exceeds HL requirements yet remains appropriate and consistently linked to the problem, evidencing expert application at the correct level.

annotations.comment: The title is concise and clearly states the research question and context, guiding the reader immediately to the focus of the exploration.
The personal anecdote provides strong engagement, but it could be more tightly linked to the mathematical aims by briefly highlighting how the childhood curiosity motivates specific model choices.
The aims are comprehensive but densely worded. Consider listing the subâaims as bullet points to improve conciseness and allow quick reference to each objective.
The rationale effectively links layer doubling and sideâlength halving to geometric sequences. Introducing the term âgeometric sequenceâ earlier would improve mathematical clarity.
While Figure 2 visually supports the explanation, the diagram lacks scale labels or dimension markers. Adding these would strengthen the link between the visual and algebraic description.
The description of alternating fold directions is clear and wellâillustrated, enhancing reader understanding through precise mathematical language.
In Figure 4 caption, the subscript in âl2â is rendered inconsistently as HTML. Use consistent LaTeX or proper subscript notation (e.g. $$l_2$$) for precision.

Introducing the ideal semicircular fold is a good modelling choice, but the transition to an elliptical model is only mentioned verbally. Consider deriving the ellipse arc length formula to extend the model.
The equation $$C = \pi r$$ is accurate, but a brief note on the units and its derivation from the semicircle would reinforce understanding.
The assumption $$r = \tfrac{1}{2}t$$ is clearly stated, but defining the variable $$t$$ (e.g. $$t_n$$ for thickness after $$n$$ folds) would prevent ambiguity later.
The derivation of $$C = \tfrac{\pi}{2}t$$ is correctly stated, but you could emphasize that $$t$$ doubles with each fold by writing $$t_n = 2^n t_0$$ at this point.
Section 2.1 is wellâplaced and signals a shift from background to analysis, aiding the logical structure and helping the reader navigate the document.
The recursive definition $$t_1 = 2t_0$$ is communicated clearly using consistent notation, effectively reinforcing the doubling thickness concept.

The algebraic expansion in $$l_1 = \frac{l_0}{2} - \frac{\pi}{4}2t_0 = \frac{l_0 - \frac{\pi}{4}t_0\cdot 2^2}{2}$$ is correct but may obscure the pattern. Consider factoring powers of two earlier to highlight the sequence.
For $$w_1 = \frac{w_0 - \frac{\pi}{4}2^2t_0}{2}$$ consider showing the intermediate substitution of $$C_2=\tfrac{\pi}{2}t_2$$ explicitly to reinforce the induction basis.
Table 1 effectively summarizes the expressions for decreased lengths, but consider centering column headings and indicating units (e.g. cm) to enhance clarity.
The general formula for a geometric series $$S_n=\frac{u_1(r^n-1)}{r-1}$$ is correctly stated, showing strong command of series theory.
The induction proof narrative is solid, but numbering each step (base case, inductive hypothesis, inductive step) explicitly would enhance mathematical communication clarity.
The manipulation in combining denominators (Equation 13) is rigorous, but briefly stating âcommon denominator 2^{x+1}â before combining would guide the reader through the step.
The multiâline step solving for $$l_0$$ is dense. Breaking this into two or three smaller displayed equations would improve readability and reduce cognitive load.
The final expression $$k=\tfrac13\log_2\bigl(\frac{28l_0/\pi t_0+4}{4}\bigr)$$ is clearly stated, allowing immediate interpretation of parameter effects.
Table 2 presents widths and general expressions effectively, but adding a brief legend explaining $$h$$ and $$w_h$$ would help the reader unfamiliar with previous sections.
Including units (cm) inside the argument of logarithms in the computation of $$k$$ may confuse; clarify that units cancel or convert to dimensionless form before logging.
Applying the formula for $$h$$ to real data showcases strong mathematical application and confirms the modelâs robustness in practice.
Table 4âs comparison of calculated and experimental folds is comprehensive and supports validation; the inclusion of both odd and even cases highlights consistency.
Figures 13â15 are informative but share one caption. Label each subfigure (a), (b), (c) and assign individual captions to maintain clarity in multiâimage sets.
The conclusion succinctly restates the core findings and links back to the aims, demonstrating coherent reflection on the investigationâs objectives.
The evaluation notes model limitations but could deepen by outlining specific future extensions, such as parameter sensitivity analysis or alternative fold geometries.
